titleStmt.title: Letter from James Patrick Farrell to Augustine Birrell, 27 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Patrick Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from the desk of the North Longford MP James Patrick Farrell (1865-1921) to that of Chief Secretary of Ireland Augustine Birrell (1850-1933). Farrell is writing the letter with the intended purpose of giving his backing to Mr Thomas Delany (1868-1939) and his application to become the new chief crown solicitor of Ireland, following the sudden death of Sir Malachy Kelly (1851-1916). Mr Delany, the crown solicitor for Longford, was Farrell's first preference for the position and he attempted to convince Birrell of this in the letter.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6258/7

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Longford March, 27th. 1916 To The Right Hon. Augustine Birrell, M.P. Chief Secretsry for Ireland. Dear Mr. Birrell-: You will remember on one occasion
some three years ago when I had the honour of introduc-
ing to you in London Mr. Thomas W. Delany, Crown
Solicitor for the County of Longford, who was then
seeking the position of Under Secretary for Ireland. A
position however has now become vacant by the death of
Sir. Malachy Kelly which would far more suit Mr. Delany
and I understand that he is making application for it
to-day. I need not enumerate the many public positions
which Mr. Delany occupies here, in all of which by his
ability and tact he is conferring inestimable services
on the public affairs people of this County. If I were in your
great position, I would be glad to secure the services of
such a man as Mr. Delany, and with the greatest
confidence I appeal to you to see that before the appoint-
ment is made. due regard is had to the ability, industry
and influence of Mr. Delany.
I may mention that since the War broke out, he has
done most valuable work here for Recruiting. In fact
I doubt if the results of the appeals for men to fill
the gaps of Irish Regiments. shown in County Longford,
would have been anything equal to what they are were
it not for his great services. Your department has now an opportunity
of recognising those services and I sincerely hope
you will avail of it. Faithfully yours, James P. Farrell
